author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council
book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XCI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Those who give drugs for procuring abortion, and those who receive poisons to kill the fœtus, are subjected to the penalty of murder.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Whoever gives or receives medicine to produce abortion is a homicide.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: See Canon XXI. of Ancyra, and Canon II. of St. Basil; to wit, “She who purposely destroys the fœtus, shall suffer the punishment of murder.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: And we pay no attention to the subtile distinction as to whether the fœtus was formed or unformed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: And by this not only is justice satisfied for the child that should have been born, but also for her who prepared for herself the snares, since the women very often die who make such experiments.”
